reasoning: The evidence strongly suggests the US will not allow any chips better than the H200 (such as Blackwell B100/B200/B30A) to be exported to China before March 14, 2026. Even the export of the H200, which President Trump approved in late 2025 (subject to a surcharge), has been fraught with difficulties, including pushback from US lawmakers and reports in Jan-Feb 2026 that Chinese customs authorities have blocked H200 shipments. The China-specific B30A Blackwell derivative chip was explicitly declined by the administration in late 2025. With only two weeks left before the resolution date, neither side has demonstrated the political will—nor is there any reported fast-tracked negotiation underway—to approve or successfully ship any more advanced chips. The probability is effectively as low as possible.
